title: Why CoT Boosts Accuracy
concept: By generating intermediate reasoning tokens, the model gets more compute steps (one forward pass per token) to process complex relationships before making the final decision.

exercises.prompt: Why does writing out reasoning step-by-step help an AI get the right answer?

options.text: It gives the model more compute steps to process complex relationships.
It physically changes the user's prompt text.
It prevents the model from writing any numbers.

exercises.explain: By writing out intermediate reasoning steps, the model gets more calculation cycles (tokens) to work out complex logic.

cards.title: Thinking in a Flash
cards.body: Have you ever tried to solve a complex puzzle in your head in under one second? It's really hard! AI models face the same issue when forced to answer instantly. Giving them time to 'think out loud' changes everything.

cards.title: Tokens as Thinking Steps
cards.body: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) boosts accuracy because LLMs generate text token by token. Every single token generated requires a full math cycle (a forward pass). By writing out its thinking step-by-step, the model gets more compute steps to solve complex relationships before arriving at the final answer.

cards.title: The Scratchpad Analogy
cards.body: Imagine you are asked to solve a tricky multi-step math word problem. If you must shout out the answer instantly, you might slip up. But if you are allowed to write down your scratch work step-by-step, you get to think through each part before writing the final number. Chain-of-Thought is the model's scratchpad!

cards.title: CoT in Action
cards.body:
If you ask an AI: 'If a tree has 10 apples and 3 fall, how many are left?'
Without CoT, it might guess '7' instantly, but fail on harder math. 
With CoT, it generates: 'First, count the total: 10. Next, subtract the fallen: 3. 10 minus 3 is 7. Answer: 7.' 
Each word it wrote gave it another moment to calculate correctly!

cards.title: Myth: All Answers Take Equal Effort
cards.body: Some people think LLMs use the same amount of effort to answer 'Yes' as they do to write a 500-word essay. Actually, more tokens generated means more computational steps! The longer reasoning path is what unlocks the correct answer.

cards.title: Direct vs. Chain-of-Thought
cards.body: See how the computation changes when we allow the model to use Chain-of-Thought versus demanding an immediate response.

left.text: Only 1 forward pass to jump straight from question to final answer. Higher chance of error.

right.text: Multiple forward passes (one per intermediate token) to process reasoning before the final answer.

exercises.prompt: How does generating intermediate reasoning tokens provide more computation to an LLM?

options.text: It bypasses the neural network completely.
It gives the model one forward pass of computation per generated token.
It makes the model run backwards through its training data.
It reduces the number of mathematical operations.

exercises.explain: Each token generated requires one forward pass of the neural network. Generating more tokens means running more mathematical steps, which helps handle complex logic.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Giving a model more steps to 'think' step-by-step allows it to perform more total computational operations before answering.
exercises.explain: Yes! Because each token generated requires a forward pass, a longer reasoning path physically translates to more computation steps.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to explain how Chain-of-Thought grants more computing power to the model.
exercises.sentence: Generating intermediate tokens gives the model more forward passes to calculate the final answer.
exercises.explain: Every extra token the model outputs requires a new forward pass, giving the model more math cycles to figure out the right answer.

exercises.prompt: Which AI response shows the model using Chain-of-Thought to boost its accuracy?

options.text: AI Response: 'You start with 10. Eating 2 leaves 8. Giving away 3 leaves 5. The answer is 5.'
AI Response: 'I cannot answer this math question.'

exercises.explain: Option B represents Chain-of-Thought reasoning. It spells out the intermediate math steps ('10 - 2 = 8', '8 - 3 = 5') instead of just jumping to the final number.

exercises.prompt: Why does generating intermediate reasoning tokens actually increase a model's accuracy on complex tasks?

options.text: It buys more computing steps (one forward pass per token) to process details before deciding.
It physically changes the weights of the AI while it is generating the answer.
It decreases the number of steps so the model can answer faster.
It allows the AI to search the live web for the correct answer.

exercises.explain: Chain-of-Thought works because it converts a hard single-step jump into a series of smaller, token-by-token forward passes, giving the model more computational time to resolve complex connections before committing to a final answer.
